label: Arduino Uno R3 (ELEGOO)
note: ATmega328P-based Uno R3 clone — used across BLExAR's RFID, GPS, and joystick hardware builds. Also the usual microcontroller for reading an IMU's raw quaternion over I2C.

label: Arduino Nano ESP32
note: Official Arduino board (ABX00083) — Bluetooth/WiFi capable, used for BLExAR's e-paper and BLE prototyping.

label: Raspberry Pi 4 Model B (4GB) Kit
note: 4GB Pi 4 kit with case/fan — the compute base for BLExAR's LiDAR, thermal, GPS, and audio-array builds.

label: Raspberry Pi Pico
note: RP2040 board used for the WS2812 LED ring and OLED display builds.

label: MFRC522 RFID Reader Kit (12pc)
note: RFID reader + tag kit used in BLExAR's Arduino RFID build.

label: TF-Luna Mini LiDAR Module
note: Single-point LiDAR module used for BLExAR's distance-detection build.

label: AMG8833 Thermal Camera (Adafruit)
note: Grid-EYE 8x8 thermal array used in BLExAR's thermal-camera build.

label: MLX90640 Thermal Camera (32x24)
note: Higher-resolution thermal array used as an alternative sensor across BLExAR's thermal and audio-array builds.

label: MLX90614 IR Temperature Sensor (GY-906)

label: NEMA 17 Stepper Motor (17HS4023)
note: Stepper motor used in BLExAR's Raspberry Pi motor-control build.

label: DRV8825 Stepper Motor Driver
note: Driver board paired with the NEMA 17 in the stepper-motor build.

label: NEO-6M GPS Module
note: GPS module used across BLExAR's GPS tracker and audio-array builds.

label: ATGM336H Mini GPS Module
note: Compact dual-mode GPS module used for BLExAR's pocket GPS tracker build.

label: MG90S Micro Servo
note: Micro servo used across several BLExAR Arduino builds.

label: SSD1306 OLED Display (I2C, 0.96")
note: Small OLED display used in BLExAR's Pico display build.

label: ADS1115 16-bit ADC Module
note: Analog-to-digital converter used across BLExAR's sensor builds.

label: WS2812B Addressable RGB LEDs
note: Individually-addressable LEDs used in BLExAR's Pico LED ring build.

label: Micro SD Card Module (Arduino)
note: SD card module used for on-device logging in BLExAR's GPS and solar-panel builds.

label: Waveshare 1.54" e-Paper Display
note: Low-power e-paper display used in BLExAR's Bluetooth e-paper build.

label: ESP32 D1 Mini Dev Board
note: Compact ESP32 board used for BLExAR's haptic-feedback joystick build.

label: Seeed Studio XIAO nRF52840
note: Tiny nRF52840 BLE board used for BLExAR's Bluetooth prototyping.

label: MPU9250 9-DoF IMU
note: 9-DoF IMU used in BLExAR's calibration builds. The onboard magnetometer is a 3-axis Hall-effect sensor — the same physics modeled by the magnetic field-line tracer — while the DMP outputs attitude as a quaternion matching CMHeadphoneMotionManager's format.

label: INA226 Current/Voltage Sensor
note: Power-monitoring module used in BLExAR's solar-panel characterization build.

label: Potentiometer Kit
note: Used as a rheostat in BLExAR's solar-panel characterization build.

label: Breadboard (400 Tie Points)
note: Standard prototyping breadboard used across BLExAR builds.

label: SanDisk Ultra 16GB microSD
note: Storage card used in BLExAR's solar-panel data-logging build.

label: Small Solar Panel (2V, 120mA)
note: 54x54mm panel used in BLExAR's solar characterization build.

label: 2-Axis Analog Joystick Module
note: Joystick module used in BLExAR's haptic-feedback joystick build.

label: 3.7V Vibration Motor
note: Haptic motor used in BLExAR's joystick build.

label: 3/4" Hall Effect Flow Meter
note: Flow sensor used in BLExAR's water-metering build. Uses a Hall-effect magnetic sensor — the same Biot–Savart field the tracer models.

label: 3.7V LiPo Battery (600mAh)
note: Rechargeable battery used across portable BLExAR builds.

label: 12V 2A Power Supply
note: Power supply used for BLExAR's stepper-motor build.

label: 5V 3A USB-C Power Supply
note: Power supply used for Raspberry Pi builds and Biquadia's studio desk.

label: Behringer UMC1820
note: Audio interface used building Biquadia — 8-preamp USB I/O for real-time DSP testing.

label: USB Sound Card
note: USB audio interface used in early Biquadia MEMS-mic prototyping.

label: MEMS Microphone Breakout
note: MEMS mic breakout used for real-time DSP experiments feeding into Biquadia.

label: IK Multimedia iRig Pro Duo
note: Portable 2-channel USB-C audio interface used for mobile Biquadia field recording.

label: Shure MV88+ Video Kit
note: Portable stereo condenser mic kit used for Biquadia field/video capture.

label: Shure MV7
note: USB/XLR hybrid mic used building and testing nymic.

label: Blue Yeti Nano
note: Compact USB condenser mic used for nymic testing.

label: Rode NT-USB+
note: USB condenser mic used for nymic testing.

label: Audio-Technica ATH-M50x
note: Reference monitoring headphones used for akous's binaural audio testing.

label: Sony MDR-7506
note: Reference studio headphones used for akous's binaural audio testing.

label: Sennheiser Wireless Microphone System
note: Wireless mic system used for akous's ambient/binaural field recording.

label: Apple AirPods Pro (3rd gen)
note: MotionLink's head-tracking feature depends on the Headphone Motion API, which requires AirPods Pro.

label: Deep Learning (Goodfellow, Bengio, Courville)
note: Foundational deep-learning textbook referenced while building itria.

label: Hands-On Machine Learning (Aurélien Géron)
note: Practical ML reference used while building itria.

label: Designing Machine Learning Systems (Chip Huyen)
note: ML systems-design reference used while building itria.

label: SanDisk Extreme Portable SSD (1TB)
note: Portable SSD used for studio project storage and backups.

label: USB-C Multiport Hub/Dock
note: Studio desk dock used across all app development work.

label: Adafruit BNO055 9-DOF Absolute Orientation IMU
note: Bosch BNO055 with on-chip sensor fusion outputting a unit quaternion directly — the exact quaternion this converter round-trips into yaw/pitch/roll, same data CMHeadphoneMotionManager delivers.

label: 3D Math Primer for Graphics and Game Development (2nd)
note: Chapters 8-10 derive quaternion multiplication, Euler-angle gimbal lock, and the conversion matrix this playground verifies live — the same math driving MotionLink's head-pose.

label: Precision Metal Gyroscope Spinner
note: Spinning rotor demonstrating angular momentum conservation and precession — the physical intuition behind why quaternions avoid gimbal lock where Euler angles fail.

label: Nonlinear Dynamics and Chaos (Strogatz, 3rd)
note: Chapter 6 explicitly integrates the double pendulum Lagrangian — Lyapunov exponent, phase-space Poincaré section, and the sensitivity that this simulator paints as geometry.

label: Chaos: Making a New Science (Gleick)
note: Narrative history of Lorenz, Feigenbaum, and the butterfly effect — the same deterministic chaos this double pendulum exhibits when you nudge its Lyapunov divergence slider.

label: Kinetic Chaos Pendulum Desk Sculpture
note: Double oloid chaos pendulum whose aperiodic motion never repeats — a physical embodiment of the Lagrangian symplectic integration this page runs at 4th order.

label: Classical Mechanics (Goldstein, 3rd)
note: Canonical derivation of the double-pendulum equations of motion via Euler-Lagrange — exactly the T-V Lagrangian this simulator integrates symplectically.

label: Visual Complex Analysis (Needham, 25th Anniversary)
note: Entire premise is epicycles-as-complex-exponentials — e^{iwt} spinning phasors whose sum is a discrete Fourier series, same DFT this playground draws with.

label: Signals and Systems (Oppenheim, 2nd)
note: Defines the DTFT, DFT, and complex Fourier coefficients c_k this visualizer computes — chapters 3-5 derive the exact reconstruction formula tracing your doodle.

label: Understanding Digital Signal Processing (Lyons, 3rd)
note: Chapter on DFT and windowing explains spectral leakage and Gibbs phenomenon visible as overshoot when you draw sharp corners in the epicycles tracer.

label: Oscilloscope + Function Generator Kit (2-channel)
note: Feed a square wave into your scope and see its Fourier epicycles live — same harmonic decomposition (odd k only) this page animates as rotating vectors.

label: Introduction to Electrodynamics (Griffiths, 4th)
note: Chapter 5 derives Biot-Savart law and Helmholtz coil field — the exact integral this tracer evaluates with RK4 field-line marching.

label: Magnetic Field Viewing Film 4x4 inch
note: Green film renders flux lines from Biot-Savart superposition visible — lay it over a current loop and see the same dipole field this simulator traces via Runge-Kutta.

label: CMS Magnetics Green Magnetic Viewing Film 4x6
note: Shows Helmholtz pair uniformity and solenoid fringe fields in real life — directly comparable to the B_z magnitude readout under your cursor.

label: Gauss Meter Teslameter (Hall-Effect)
note: Measures B-field magnitude around loops/solenoids — hand-verify the Biot-Savart μ0/4π scaling this tracer computes before field-line integration.

label: The Lattice Boltzmann Method: Principles and Practice (Krüger)
note: Defines the D2Q9 equilibrium distribution f_i^eq = w_i ρ [1 + c_i·u/cs² ...] and BGK collision operator this tunnel implements live for Von Kármán shedding.

label: An Introduction to Fluid Dynamics (Batchelor)
note: Derives Navier-Stokes and Reynolds number Re = UL/ν — crank Re past ~50 in this LBM tunnel and watch the von Kármán vortex street Batchelor describes appear.

label: Eisco Complete Optical Bench Set (40pc)
note: Actually a wind-tunnel-friendly flow-viz partner — pair with smoke tube to see boundary-layer separation and wake shedding your D2Q9 simulation traces numerically.

label: Desktop Wind Tunnel Kit + Flow Visualization Smoke
note: Physical counterpart to this D2Q9 LBM solver — place cylinder or airfoil in the stream and compare real vortex shedding Strouhal frequency to the simulation's vorticity field.

label: Optics (Hecht, 5th Edition)
note: Chapter 6 derives the 2×2 ABCD matrix for lenses/mirrors — the exact paraxial ray transfer this bench composes to build system matrix and effective focal length.

label: Optics Global Edition (Hecht)
note: Covers g1·g2 resonator stability criterion |g1 g2| ≤ 1 — toggle the two-mirror cavity in this bench and watch stable/unstable regions match Hecht's diagram.

label: Physics Optics Experiment Kit (Convex/Concave Lens Set)
note: Handheld concave/convex lenses and ray box — build the same ABCD cascade (lens-gap-mirror) physically and verify the numerical ray fan traced here.

label: Optical Bench Rail with Lens and Mirror Set
note: 1-meter rail with carriers for lenses/mirrors — recreate the resonator stability test g1·g2 and image formation this simulator ray-traces via ABCD multiplication.

label: Introduction to Quantum Mechanics (Griffiths, 3rd)
note: Chapters 2-3 cover time-independent Schrödinger equation, rectangular barrier, and exact transmission T = [1 + V0² sinh²(kL)/4E(V0-E)]⁻¹ that this Crank-Nicolson run checks live.

label: Principles of Quantum Mechanics (Shankar, 2nd)
note: Derives unitary evolution and Crank-Nicolson as Cayley form (I - iHΔt/2)/(I + iHΔt/2) preserving norm — the method this simulator uses for wave-packet tunneling.

label: Modern Quantum Mechanics (Sakurai, 2nd)
note: Chapter 2 solves barrier penetration via WKB and exact matching — launch a Gaussian packet in this page and compare measured T/R against Sakurai's analytic formula.

label: Double-Slit Quantum Eraser Experiment Kit
note: Physical demonstration of matter-wave interference underlying Schrödinger's equation — same wave-particle duality that makes tunneling through a classically forbidden barrier possible.

label: Complex Analysis (Ahlfors, 3rd)
note: Defines analytic functions, Cauchy-Riemann ∂u/∂x = ∂v/∂y, and conformal property — drag f(z)=z² and watch CR fail/preserve angles live as Ahlfors proves.

label: Complex Variables and Applications (Churchill & Brown, 9th)
note: Chapter on mapping w = e^z, log z, Joukowski transform — the same homotopy slider f(z,t) this explorer interpolates between identity and complex map.

label: Visual Differential Geometry and Forms (Needham)
note: Sequel to Visual Complex Analysis — explains how conformal warp preserves infinitesimal circles, which you see as grid squares stay square under holomorphic f(z).

label: GSC Standing Wave Demonstration Kit
note: Driver + square plate kit — sweep frequency and see nodal lines accumulate sand along Chladni figures exactly as this modal eigensolver's Laplacian eigenmodes predict.

label: 3B Scientific Metal Square Chladni Plate 7 inch
note: Precision steel plate for cymatics — dust with fine sand and drive at computed eigenfrequency to realize the Bessel-function nodal patterns this simulator solves.

label: Ernst Chladni Professional Vibration Generator (30Hz-20kHz)
note: 30W driver sweeping through plate resonances — matches the eigenvalue sweep in this Chladni lab where sand flees antinodes to settle on nodal lines.

label: Toysmith Euler Disk
note: Spinning disk's decreasing contact and rising audible frequency demonstrate modal energy concentration — same standing-wave energy argument explaining why Chladni sand avoids vibrating regions.

label: Master Handbook of Acoustics (Everest & Pohlmann, 7th)
note: Bible of RT60, Sabine, and absorption coefficients — the exact formulas these acoustic calculators implement for reverb, room modes, and critical distance.

label: miniDSP UMIK-1 USB Measurement Microphone
note: Calibrated USB mic with individual cal file — measure your room's RT60 and modal peaks to validate the room-mode eigenfrequencies this calculator predicts.

label: Dayton Audio EMM-6 Measurement Microphone
note: Omni condenser with cal file — pair with REW to measure SPL, RT60, and comb filtering the acoustic calculator suite computes via image-source method.

label: Behringer ECM8000 Measurement Microphone
note: Reference omni for acoustic measurement — captures the same pressure field these fast calculators approximate with ray-tracing and modal sums.

label: Loudspeaker Design Cookbook (Dickason, 7th)
note: Breakup modes, cone edge termination, and waveguide directivity index — chapters 7-9 explain the cone-breakup visualization and waveguide polar math this lab implements.

label: Testing Loudspeakers (D'Appolito)
note: Step-by-step measurement of Thiele-Small, K_ms(x), Le(x) — same nonlinearities the loudspeaker nonlinearity lab models with displacement-dependent Bl(x) and stiffness.

label: Dayton Audio UMM-6 USB Measurement Microphone
note: USB cal mic for nearfield SPL and cone-breakup waterfall — capture the CSD breakup modes this simulator predicts via modal superposition.

label: Ferrofluid Display Bottle with Neodymium Magnets
note: Ferrofluid spikes under magnetic field emulate ferrofluid cooling in voice-coil gaps — the same thermal-magnetic mechanism reducing thermal compression via enhanced heat transfer.

label: CMS Magnetics Ferrofluid 2oz Science Kit
note: Lab-grade ferrofluid showing thermomagnetic convection — pour near a voice-coil mock-up and see why vendors add ferrofluid to lower R_th and Ti rise.

label: FLIR Thermal Camera iPhone Attachment
note: IR camera imaging voice-coil heating ΔT = P * R_th — directly visualize the copper TCR and thermal compression curve this lab simulates.

label: K-Type Thermocouple Thermometer (Dual Channel)
note: Probe voice-coil former temperature and log T_vc(t) — compare measured τ_thermal rise against this lab's first-order thermal RC model with ferrofluid cooling.

label: Heat and Mass Transfer (Cengel, 6th)
note: Derives conduction, convection, R_th = L/kA, and thermal time constant — the exact lumped thermal network this voice-coil compression simulator integrates.

label: Digital Signal Processing (Proakis & Manolakis, 5th)
note: Chapter on biquad Direct Form II transposed and pole-zero placement — move poles in pole-zero explorer and hear Q/ω0 map to ring frequency as Proakis derives.

label: Teensy 4.0 + Audio Shield (DSP Dev Kit)
note: 600 MHz M7 with SGTL5000 codec — flash the DF2T biquad cascade from Biquadia's DSP core and hear the same filter your design exports here.

label: DAFX: Digital Audio Effects (Zölzer, 2nd)
note: Biquad-based effects: peaking, shelving, allpass for phaser — agentic pipeline's RAG over DAFX generates the same C++ kernel clang++ verifies here.

label: The Scientist and Engineer's Guide to DSP (Smith)
note: Intuitive pole-zero explanation — why a pole near unit circle rings, zero near kills — the exact intuition this pole-zero explorer makes tangible with drag-to-listen.

label: Orrery Mechanical Solar System Model (Brass)
note: Brass gears showing Keplerian ellipses and Lagrange points — same Newtonian gravity μ/r² this N-body choreography integrates with symplectic Yoshida 4th order.

label: Orbital Mechanics for Engineering Students (Curtis, 4th)
note: Derives n-body equations, figure-eight choreography existence proof, and symplectic energy conservation — exactly the trajectory this page verifies stays bounded for 500 periods.

label: Newton's Cradle Balance Balls (Large, 7-ball)
note: Conservation of momentum/energy demo — the same conservation your symplectic integrator preserves while Newtonian gravity chaotically exchanges momentum among three equal masses.

label: Gravitational N-Body Simulations (Aarseth)
note: Direct N-body with Hermite integration — compares to this page's symplectic scheme showing why 4th-order composition keeps figure-eight orbit stable vs Euler blow-up.

label: Spacetime Physics (Taylor & Wheeler, 2nd)
note: Defines hyperbolic worldline x = c²/a (cosh aτ/c -1), proper time, and Minkowski diagram — the exact equations this starship journey plots as you flip-and-burn to Proxima.

label: LEGO Apollo Saturn V 92176
note: 1:110 Saturn V with 1969 Apollo stages — build it while your virtual ship undergoes Lorentz contraction and relativistic Doppler shift of CMB to visible blue.

label: Estes Alpha III Model Rocket Kit
note: Real rocket with 1g thrust-to-weight teaching proper acceleration — contrast with this page's constant 1g proper-acceleration hyperbolic motion where coordinate acceleration → 0 near c.

label: Relativity: The Special and General Theory (Einstein)
note: Einstein's own derivation of Lorentz transformation and Minkowski spacetime — the same γ = 1/√(1-β²) this page computes to show ship time vs Earth time twin paradox.

label: Satellite Orbits: Models, Methods and Applications (Montenbruck)
note: SGP4 and TLE propagation — this globe computes ISS position client-side from real TLE elements using the same Kepler + J2 math Montenbruck derives.

label: Metal Earth ISS 3D Metal Model Kit
note: Steel ISS model — track the real ISS live on this globe's TLE orbit, then hold the model while it passes over your city at 7.66 km/s orbital velocity.

label: Illuminated Desktop World Globe (12 inch)
note: Backlit globe with constellation overlay — compare its static terminator to this page's dynamically computed solar declination and real-time weather overlay across 16 cities.

label: MOVA Globe Satellite View with Cloud Cover (4.5 inch)
note: Solar-powered, self-rotating satellite globe that spins continuously using ambient light and Earth's magnetic field — demonstrating Coriolis torque and gyroscopic rotation live.

label: nRF52840 DK Eval Board (Nordic PCA10056)
note: Official nRF52840 dev kit with BLE 5 and Thread — flash a GATT server and watch this explorer parse your advertised services/characteristics live.

label: Arduino Nano 33 BLE Rev2 (nRF52840)
note: nRF52840 board running ArduinoBLE — same chipset family as XIAO nRF52840 in BLExAR builds, exposing GATT structure this page visualizes as byte-level CSV frames.

label: Bluetooth Low Energy: The Developer's Handbook (Heydon)
note: GATT, ATT, L2CAP, and UUID breakdown — chapter 4 maps exactly to the service/characteristic tree this visualizer renders for HM-10 UART bridge.

label: nRF52840 USB Dongle BLE Sniffer (PCA10059)
note: Wireshark BLE sniffer capturing over-the-air GATT transactions — see raw ATT Read/Write that become the CSV bytes this explorer frames.

label: 3Dio Free Space Binaural Microphone
note: Dummy-head binaural mic capturing HRTF — spatial cues this head-tracked stereo panner approximates via ITD/ILD and quaternion yaw driving StereoPannerNode.

label: Roland CS-10EM Binaural Microphones
note: In-ear binaural mics for personal HRTF — record your own pinna response then compare panning when you rotate the virtual head using gyroscope yaw.

label: Immersive Sound: The Art and Science of Binaural and Multi-Channel Audio (Roginska/Geluso)
note: Derives HRTF, ITD = d/c sinθ, and head-tracking compensation — the exact panning law this playground interpolates as you drag yaw.

label: BNO055 Head Tracker IMU (Wired USB)
note: 9-axis sensor streaming quaternion yaw for spatial audio — plug its fused heading into this stereo panner's yaw input and hear source stay world-locked as you turn.

label: TinyML (Warden & Situnayake)
note: Quantization-aware training fp32→int8 and model footprint math — same byte-size arithmetic bytes = params * bits/8 this calculator does for CoreML fp16/int4.

label: NVIDIA Jetson Nano Developer Kit (4GB)
note: Edge GPU where int8 quantized models from this calculator actually run — compare theoretical size saving vs measured latency drop on Jetson vs iPhone Neural Engine.

label: Coral USB Accelerator (Edge TPU)
note: USB Edge TPU for int8 quantized nets — run the same quantized CoreML model sized here and see why int8 cuts RAM bandwidth but needs per-channel scales.

label: Efficient Deep Learning (Menghani)
note: Covers fp16, int8, int4 quantization schemes and Huffman overhead — formulas this calculator implements to compute exact on-device footprint without throughput guesswork.

label: NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano Super Developer Kit
note: 67 TOPS edge AI dev kit — benchmark int4 quantized models sized here and validate that CoreML quantized size math predicts actual flash/RAM usage on device.

label: The Physics of Musical Instruments (Fletcher & Rossing, 2nd)
note: Modal analysis of plates and strings with Bessel functions — root of Chladni plate eigenmodes and waveguide modal cutoff fc = c/2a this toolbox computes.

label: Acoustic Foam Panels 24-pack Pyramid 2 inch
note: Pyramid foam taming room modes at f = n c / 2L — measure RT60 drop and compare to acoustic-calculator predictions for absorption coefficient vs frequency.

label: Acoustic Foam Panels 50-pack Wedge 1 inch
note: 1-inch wedge for mid-high absorption — use with room-mode calculator's Sabine RT60 equation to predict reverberation time reduction.

label: Acoustic Foam 24-pack 2 inch Charcoal
note: Dense foam damping axial modes — place at pressure antinodes displayed in room-mode visualizer's 3D pressure field.

label: Dayton Audio EMM-6 Omnidirectional Measurement Mic (New)
note: Latest EMM-6 variant with calibration file — measure waveguide polar response and cone-breakup waterfall to validate directivity index this lab computes.

label: Dayton Audio UMM-6 USB Measurement Microphone (Legacy)
note: Legacy USB measurement mic for REW — capture on-axis SPL and compare to enclosure designer frequency response with baffle diffraction.

label: Dayton Audio iMM-6C USB-C Measurement Mic
note: USB-C calibrated mic for iPhone/iPad — take to listening room and verify room-mode eigenfrequencies against calculator's mode list up to 300Hz.

label: Jetson Nano Developer Kit 16GB eMMC
note: eMMC variant for TinyML deployment — flash int8 quantized CoreML model sized by this calculator and measure flash vs RAM footprint tradeoff.

label: GeeekPi nRF52840 Micro Dev Kit USB Dongle
note: nRF52840 dongle acting as BLE sniffer — captures ATT MTU and GATT notifications that become CSV rows this BLE visualizer frames.

label: Fundamentals of Photonics (Saleh & Teich, 2nd)
note: Derives Gaussian beam resonator stability and ABCD law for laser cavities — test the g1·g2 ≤1 condition this bench evaluates for two-mirror resonators.

label: Principles of Optics (Born & Wolf, 7th)
note: Rigorous electromagnetic theory of ABCD and Fresnel diffraction — compare paraxial ray fan this bench traces to Born's exact wavefront aberrations.

label: Turbulent Flows (Pope)
note: Kolmogorov energy cascade and vortex stretching — crank Reynolds number in LBM tunnel past transition and see Pope's -5/3 law emerge in vorticity power spectrum.

label: Fluid Mechanics (Frank White, 8th)
note: Boundary layer separation and drag coefficient Cd vs Re — place cylinder in LBM tunnel and validate wake Strouhal against White's drag crisis curve.
